qtype: Percentage tax, percentage increase, compound interest calculation, fraction of an amount, percentage of total.
tex:
(a) In 2018, Gretal earned \$$32\,000$.
\newline

(i) (b) She paid tax of 24\% on these earnings.
\newline

Work out the amount she paid in tax in 2018.
\newline

(c) In 2019, Gretal's earnings increased by 7\%.
\newline

Work out her earnings in 2019.
\newline

(d) Gretal invests \$5000 at a rate of 2\% per year compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the value of her investment at the end of 3 years.
\newline

(e) One month, Gretal spent a total of \$360 on presents.
\newline
She spent $\dfrac{1}{5}$ of this total on presents for her parents.
\newline
She spent $\dfrac{2}{3}$ of the remaining money on presents for her friends.
\newline
She spent the rest of the money on presents for her sisters.
\newline

Calculate the percentage of the \$360 that she spent on presents for her sisters.
\newline

(f) Arjun earned \$$36\,515$ in 2019.
\newline
This was an increase of 9\% on his earnings in 2018.
\newline

Work out his earnings in 2018.
\newline

(g) Arjun and Gretal each pay rent.
\newline

In 2018, the ratio of the amount each paid in rent was Arjun : Gretal = $5 : 7$.
\newline
In 2019, the ratio of the amount each paid in rent was Arjun : Gretal = $9 : 13$.
\newline

Arjun paid the same amount of rent in both 2018 and 2019.
\newline
Gretal paid \$290 more rent in 2019 than she did in 2018.
\newline

Work out the amount Arjun paid in rent in 2019.
\newline

content.content:
In 2018, Gretal earned \$$32\,000$.
\newline

She paid tax of 24\% on these earnings.
\newline

Work out the amount she paid in tax in 2018.
\newline

In 2019, Gretal's earnings increased by 7\%.
\newline

Work out her earnings in 2019.
\newline

Gretal invests \$5000 at a rate of 2\% per year compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the value of her investment at the end of 3 years.
\newline

One month, Gretal spent a total of \$360 on presents.
\newline
She spent $\dfrac{1}{5}$ of this total on presents for her parents.
\newline
She spent $\dfrac{2}{3}$ of the remaining money on presents for her friends.
\newline
She spent the rest of the money on presents for her sisters.
\newline

Calculate the percentage of the \$360 that she spent on presents for her sisters.
\newline

Arjun earned \$$36\,515$ in 2019.
\newline
This was an increase of 9\% on his earnings in 2018.
\newline

Work out his earnings in 2018.
\newline

Arjun and Gretal each pay rent.
\newline

In 2018, the ratio of the amount each paid in rent was Arjun : Gretal = $5 : 7$.
\newline
In 2019, the ratio of the amount each paid in rent was Arjun : Gretal = $9 : 13$.
\newline

Arjun paid the same amount of rent in both 2018 and 2019.
\newline
Gretal paid \$290 more rent in 2019 than she did in 2018.
\newline

Work out the amount Arjun paid in rent in 2019.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item In 2018, Gretal earned \$32\,000.

\begin{subparts}
\item She paid tax of 24\% on these earnings.

Work out the amount she paid in tax in 2018.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\item In 2019, Gretal's earnings increased by 7\%.

Work out her earnings in 2019.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\end{subparts}

\item Gretal invests \$5000 at a rate of 2\% per year compound interest.

Calculate the value of her investment at the end of 3 years.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\item One month, Gretal spent a total of \$360 on presents.
She spent $\dfrac{1}{5}$ of this total on presents for her parents.
She spent $\dfrac{2}{3}$ of the remaining money on presents for her friends.
She spent the rest of the money on presents for her sisters.

Calculate the percentage of the \$360 that she spent on presents for her sisters.

\AnswerLine[][\%]
\Marks{4}

\item Arjun earned \$36\,515 in 2019.
This was an increase of 9\% on his earnings in 2018.

Work out his earnings in 2018.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\item Arjun and Gretal each pay rent.

In 2018, the ratio of the amount each paid in rent was Arjun : Gretal = $5 : 7$.
In 2019, the ratio of the amount each paid in rent was Arjun : Gretal = $9 : 13$.

Arjun paid the same amount of rent in both 2018 and 2019.
Gretal paid \$290 more rent in 2019 than she did in 2018.

Work out the amount Arjun paid in rent in 2019.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{4}

\end{parts}

tex:
The heights, $h$ metres, of the 120 boys in an athletics club are recorded.
\newline
The table shows information about the heights of the boys.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l||c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Height ($h$ metres) & $1.3 < h \leqslant 1.4$ & $1.4 < h \leqslant 1.5$ & $1.5 < h \leqslant 1.6$ & $1.6 < h \leqslant 1.7$ & $1.7 < h \leqslant 1.8$ & $1.8 < h \leqslant 1.9$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 7 & 18 & 30 & 24 & 27 & 14 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) (i) (b) Write down the modal class.
\newline

(c) Calculate an estimate of the mean height.
\newline

(d) (i) (e) One boy is chosen at random from the club.
\newline

Find the probability that this boy has a height greater than 1.8 m.
\newline

(f) Three boys are chosen at random from the club.
\newline

Calculate the probability that one of the boys has a height greater than 1.8 m and the other two boys each have a height of 1.4 m or less.
\newline

(g) (i) (h) Use the frequency table on page 4 to complete the cumulative frequency table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l||c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Height ($h$ metres) & $h \leqslant 1.4$ & $h \leqslant 1.5$ & $h \leqslant 1.6$ & $h \leqslant 1.7$ & $h \leqslant 1.8$ & $h \leqslant 1.9$ \\
\hline
Cumulative frequency & 7 & 25 & & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) On the grid, draw a cumulative frequency diagram to show this information.
\newline

(j) Use your diagram to find an estimate for

(i) (k) the median height,

(l) the 40th percentile.
\newline

content.content:
Write down the modal class.
\newline

Calculate an estimate of the mean height.
\newline

One boy is chosen at random from the club.
\newline

Find the probability that this boy has a height greater than 1.8 m.
\newline

Three boys are chosen at random from the club.
\newline

Calculate the probability that one of the boys has a height greater than 1.8 m and the other two boys each have a height of 1.4 m or less.
\newline

Use the frequency table on page 4 to complete the cumulative frequency table.
\newline

On the grid, draw a cumulative frequency diagram to show this information.
\newline
Use your diagram to find an estimate for
the median height,

the 40th percentile.
\newline

stem.content:
The heights, $h$ metres, of the 120 boys in an athletics club are recorded.
\newline
The table shows information about the heights of the boys.
\newline

qtype: substitution, algebraic simplification, algebraic fractions, solving linear equations, indices, expanding
tex:
(a) $s = ut + \frac{1}{2} at^2$

Find the value of $s$ when $u = 5.2$, $t = 7$ and $a = 1.6$.
\newline

(b) Simplify.
\newline

(i) (c) $3a - 5b - a + 2b$

(d) $\dfrac{5}{3x} \times \dfrac{9x}{20}$

(e) Solve.
\newline

(i) (f) $\dfrac{15}{x} = - 3$

(g) $4 (5 - 3x) = 23$

(h) Simplify.
\newline
\[ (27x^9)^{\frac{2}{3}} \]

(i) Expand and simplify.
\newline
\[ (3x - 5y)(2x + y) \]

content.content:
$s = ut + \frac{1}{2} at^2$

Find the value of $s$ when $u = 5.2$, $t = 7$ and $a = 1.6$.
\newline

Simplify.
\newline
$\dfrac{5}{3x} \times \dfrac{9x}{20}$

Solve.
\newline

Simplify.
\newline
\[ (27x^9)^{\frac{2}{3}} \]

Expand and simplify.
\newline
\[ (3x - 5y)(2x + y) \]

tex:
(a) Draw the image of triangle $T$ after a reflection in the line $y = - 1$.
\newline

(b) Draw the image of triangle $T$ after a rotation through $90^{\circ}$ clockwise about $(0, 0)$.
\newline

(c) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle $T$ onto triangle $A$.
\newline

qtype: Set definition based on number properties, Venn diagrams, set intersection and complement, cardinality.
tex:
$x$ is an integer.
\newline
\[ \xi = \{x : 41 \leqslant x \leqslant 50\} \]
\[ A = \{x : x  is an odd number\} \]
\[ B = \{x : x  is a multiple of 3\} \]
\[ C = \{x : x  is a prime number\} \]

(a) Complete the Venn diagram to show this information.
\newline

(b) List the elements of
(i) (c) $A \cap C$,

(d) $(B \cup C)'$.
\newline

(e) Find $n(A \cap B \cap C)$.
\newline

content.content:
Complete the Venn diagram to show this information.
\newline
List the elements of
$A \cap C$,

$(B \cup C)'$.
\newline

Find $n(A \cap B \cap C)$.
\newline

stem.content:
$x$ is an integer.
\newline
\[ \xi = \{x : 41 \leqslant x \leqslant 50\} \]
\[ A = \{x : x \text{ is an odd number}\} \]
\[ B = \{x : x \text{ is a multiple of 3}\} \]
\[ C = \{x : x \text{ is a prime number}\} \]

qtype: forming inequalities, graphing linear inequalities, shading regions
tex:
Raheem makes baskets and mats.
\newline
Each week he makes $x$ baskets and $y$ mats.
\newline

He makes fewer than 10 mats.
\newline
The number of mats he makes is greater than or equal to the number of baskets he makes.
\newline

(a) One of the inequalities that shows this information is $y < 10$.
\newline
Write down the other inequality.
\newline

(b) He takes $2 \frac{1}{4}$ hours to make a basket and $1 \frac{1}{2}$ hours to make a mat.
\newline
Each week he works for a maximum of 22.5 hours.
\newline

Show that $3x + 2y \leqslant 30$.
\newline

(c) On the grid, draw three straight lines and shade the unwanted regions to show these inequalities.
\newline

(d) He makes \$40 profit on each basket he sells and \$28 profit on each mat he sells.
\newline

Calculate the maximum profit he can make each week.
\newline

content.content:
One of the inequalities that shows this information is $y < 10$.
\newline
Write down the other inequality.
\newline

\newline
Each week he works for a maximum of 22.5 hours.
\newline

\newline

On the grid, draw three straight lines and shade the unwanted regions to show these inequalities.
\newline

He makes \$40 profit on each basket he sells and \$28 profit on each mat he sells.
\newline

Calculate the maximum profit he can make each week.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

tex:

\newline

\newline

\newline

Find the bearing of

(i) (d) $A$ from $B$,

(e) $B$ from $C$.
\newline

(f) Mitchell takes 35 seconds to run from $A$ to $C$.
\newline

Calculate his average running speed in kilometres per hour.
\newline

(g) Calculate the shortest distance from point $B$ to $AC$.
\newline

tex:

\newline

(c) Describe fully the symmetry of the graph of $y = \sin x$ for $0^{\circ} \leqslant x \leqslant 360^{\circ}$.
\newline

\newline

\newline

tex:
The diagram shows a sector of a circle with centre $O$, radius 8 cm and sector angle $165^{\circ}$.
\newline

(a) Calculate the total perimeter of the sector.
\newline

(b) The surface area of a sphere is the same as the area of the sector.
\newline

Calculate the radius of the sphere.
\newline
[The surface area, $A$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $A = 4\pi r^2$.
\newline
]

(c) 

A cone is made from the sector by joining $OA$ to $OB$.
\newline

(i) (d) Calculate the radius, $r$, of the cone.
\newline

(e) Calculate the volume of the cone.
\newline
[The volume, $V$, of a cone with radius $r$ and height $h$ is $V = \frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h$.
\newline
]

tex:
(a) A rhombus $ABCD$ has a diagonal $AC$ where $A$ is the point $(-3, 10)$ and $C$ is the point $(4, -4)$.
\newline

(i) (b) Calculate the length $AC$.
\newline

(c) Show that the equation of the line $AC$ is $y = - 2x + 4$.
\newline

(d) Find the equation of the line $BD$.
\newline

(e) A curve has the equation $y = x^3 + 8x^2 + 5x$.
\newline

(i) (f) Work out the coordinates of the two turning points.
\newline

(g) Determine whether each of the turning points is a maximum or a minimum.
\newline
Give reasons for your answers.
\newline
